hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of National Beautician's Day, Hairspray is a fun musical comedy that celebrates the world of 1960s hairstyling and dance.
hero.blurb: With its upbeat soundtrack and energetic choreography, Hairspray is a delightful film that will leave you smiling. The cast, including John Travolta and Queen Latifah, deliver memorable performances that bring the story to life.

released.title: Evan Almighty
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Evan Almighty is a hilarious and heartwarming comedy that explores the story of a modern-day Noah's Ark.
released.blurb: With Steve Carell's signature humor and a talented supporting cast, Evan Almighty is a entertaining film that is fun for the whole family. The movie's lighthearted tone and uplifting message make it a great choice for a summer evening.

birthday.title: St. Elmo's Fire
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Chris O'Donnell, St. Elmo's Fire is a classic coming-of-age drama that explores the lives of a group of recent college graduates.
birthday.blurb: With a talented young cast, including Chris O'Donnell, Emilio Estevez, and Rob Lowe, St. Elmo's Fire is a relatable and engaging film that captures the challenges and uncertainties of transitioning into adulthood. The movie's honest portrayal of friendship and growth makes it a timeless classic.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: This infamous thriller is often cited as one of the worst films of the 2000s, with a convoluted plot and cringe-worthy performances.
awful.blurb: With a messy and implausible storyline, I Know Who Killed Me is a laughable attempt at a psychological thriller. The film's poor acting and awkward dialogue make it a so-bad-it's-good guilty pleasure.
